Hate Crime Data Visualization

# Packages
library(tidyverse)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(zoo)


Attaching package: ‘zoo’

The following objects are masked from ‘package:base’:

    as.Date, as.Date.numeric
library(lubridate)

Attaching package: ‘lubridate’

The following object is masked from ‘package:base’:

    date
library(extrafont)
Registering fonts with R
library(tmap)
library(plotly)
library(sf)
# Flooring Date by month
df_month <- df %>% mutate(Dateas = floor_date(as_date(Dateas), "month"))
# Creating count column
df_month$Count = 1
# Summarize on date (really on month/year, since data is floored on month)
df_month2 = df_month %>%
  group_by(Dateas) %>%
  summarize(Count = sum(Count)) %>%
  ungroup()
# Rename Dateas column to Date
df_month2 = df_month2 %>% rename(Date = Dateas)
# Plotting data by year/month
p <- ggplot(df_month2, aes(x=Date, y=Count)) +
  geom_bar(stat="identity") +
  ggtitle("US Reported Hate Crime Counts by Month,\n1991-2017") +
  aes(fill = Count) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colours = colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(5,"Oranges"))(5)) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "#e1dcdc"), 
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "#e1dcdc"),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent",colour = NA),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent",colour = NA),
    text=element_text(size=16, family="Assistant-Bold"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(t = 0, r = 20, b = 0, l = 0)),
    axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 20, r = 0, b = 0, l = 0)),
    plot.title = element_text(margin = margin(t = 0, r = 0, b = 20, l = 0), size=20, hjust = 0.5)
  )
p

####
### Bar plot bias motivations for Sept 2001
####
# Make month column for df
df$yearmonth = substr(df$Incident.Date, 1, 6)
# Filter to get records from Sept 2001 only
df_sept11 = df %>% filter(yearmonth == "200109")
df_sept11$Count = 1
# Summarize on bias motivations
df_sept11 = df_sept11 %>%
  group_by(Bias.Motivation) %>%
  summarize(Count = sum(Count)) %>%
  ungroup()
# Make Bias Motivation of a character class
df_sept11$Bias.Motivation = as.character(df_sept11$Bias.Motivation)
# Import Bias Motivation key
bmotiv = read.csv("Bias_Motivation_Key.csv")
# Merge key with data
df_sept11 = merge(df_sept11, bmotiv, by = "Bias.Motivation")
p2 <- ggplot(df_sept11, aes(x = reorder(Bias.Name, Count), y=Count)) +
  geom_bar(stat="identity") +
  ggtitle("Reported Hate Crime Counts by Bias Motivation,\nSeptember 2001") +
  xlab("Bias Motivation") +
  aes(fill = Count) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colours = colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(5,"Oranges"))(5)) +
  coord_flip() +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "#e1dcdc"), 
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "#e1dcdc"),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent",colour = NA),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent",colour = NA),
    text=element_text(size=16, family="Assistant-Bold"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(t = 0, r = 20, b = 0, l = 0)),
    axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 20, r = 0, b = 0, l = 0)),
    plot.title = element_text(margin = margin(t = 0, r = 0, b = 20, l = 0), size=20, hjust = 0.5)
  )
p2

####
### Bar plot - all bias motivations/counts
####
# Make month column for df
df$yearmonth = substr(df$Incident.Date, 1, 6)
# Creating count column
df$Count = 1
# Summarize on bias motivations
df_bias = df %>%
  group_by(Bias.Motivation) %>%
  summarize(Count = sum(Count)) %>%
  ungroup()
# Import Bias Motivation key
bmotiv = read.csv("Bias_Motivation_Key.csv")
# Merge key with data
df_bias = merge(df_bias, bmotiv, by = "Bias.Motivation")
p5 <- ggplot(df_bias, aes(x = reorder(Bias.Name, Count), y=Count)) +
  geom_bar(stat="identity") +
  ggtitle("Reported Hate Crime Counts by Bias Motivation,\n1991-2017") +
  xlab("Bias Motivation") +
  aes(fill = Count) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colours = colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(5,"Oranges"))(5)) +
  coord_flip() +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "#e1dcdc"), 
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "#e1dcdc"),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent",colour = NA),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent",colour = NA),
    text=element_text(size=16, family="Assistant-Bold"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(t = 0, r = 20, b = 0, l = 0)),
    axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 20, r = 0, b = 0, l = 0)),
    plot.title = element_text(margin = margin(t = 0, r = 0, b = 20, l = 0), size=20, hjust = 0.5)
  )
p5

### Violin Plot - Year and # in States
# Create count column 
df$Count = 1
# Summarize by State and Year
df1 = df %>%
  group_by(StateAbbr, Year) %>% 
  summarise(Count = sum(Count)) %>%
  ungroup()
# Change year column to character
df1$Year = as.character(df1$Year)
# Reading in key with pop estimates
key = read.csv("../5_All_Files_Merged_to_Key/All_to_Key.csv")
# Making unique columns on which to merge
key$SYr = paste0(key$StateAbbr, "_", key$Year)
df1$SYr = paste0(df1$StateAbbr, "_", df1$Year)
# Merging data with key with pop estimates
df2 = merge(df1, key, by = "SYr", all.y = TRUE)
# Selecting out needed columns
df2 = df2 %>% select(SYr, StateAbbr.y, Count, Pop_Int)
# Changing NAs to 0
df2[is.na(df2)] <- 0
# Creating Year column
df2$Year = substr(df2$SYr, 4, 7)
# Creating rate per 1 million column
df2$Rate = (df2$Count / df2$Pop_Int) * 1000000
# Create Violin Plot for Count
p3 = ggplot(df1, aes(x=Year, y=Count, fill=Year)) +
  geom_violin() +
  #scale_fill_gradientn(colours = colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(5,"Oranges"))(5)) +
  #scale_y_log10() +
  ggtitle("Distribution of Reported Hate Crime Counts by State,\n1991-2017") +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "#e1dcdc"), 
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "#e1dcdc"),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent",colour = NA),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent",colour = NA),
    text=element_text(size=16, family="Assistant-Bold"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(t = 0, r = 20, b = 0, l = 0)),
    axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 20, r = 0, b = 0, l = 0)),
    plot.title = element_text(margin = margin(t = 0, r = 0, b = 20, l = 0), size=20, hjust = 0.5)
  )
p3

# Create Violin Plot for Rate
p4 = ggplot(df2, aes(x=Year, y=Rate, fill=Year)) +
  geom_violin() +
  ylab("Rate per 1 Million People") +
  #scale_fill_gradientn(colours = colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(5,"Oranges"))(5)) +
  #scale_y_log10() +
  ggtitle("Distribution of Reported Hate Crime Rates by State,\n1991-2017") +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "#e1dcdc"), 
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "#e1dcdc"),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent",colour = NA),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent",colour = NA),
    text=element_text(size=16, family="Assistant-Bold"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(t = 0, r = 20, b = 0, l = 0)),
    axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 20, r = 0, b = 0, l = 0)),
    plot.title = element_text(margin = margin(t = 0, r = 0, b = 20, l = 0), size=20, hjust = 0.5)
  )
p4

key = read.csv("../5_All_Files_Merged_to_Key/All_to_Key.csv")
#which( colnames(key)=="P_Crim_Tot" )
dfmap = aggregate(key[,c(65)], by=list(STUSPS = key$StateAbbr), mean)
# Rename column
dfmap = dfmap %>% rename(Rate = x)
# Change ST column to character (it's a factor now)
dfmap$STUSPS = as.character(dfmap$STUSPS)
# Rename NB to NE
dfmap[27, 1] = "NE"
# Multiply rate by 1 million
dfmap$Rateper1Mil = dfmap$Rate * 1000000
# Reading in state shapefile
state.sf <- st_read("../10_Shapefiles/US_State_2018")
Reading layer `cb_2018_us_state_20m' from data source `/Users/lherbert1/Documents/0 Masters Thesis/Hate_Crime_Data/2_Created_Files/10_Shapefiles/US_State_2018' using driver `ESRI Shapefile'
Simple feature collection with 52 features and 9 fields
geometry type:  MULTIPOLYGON
dimension:      XY
bbox:           xmin: -179.1743 ymin: 17.91377 xmax: 179.7739 ymax: 71.35256
epsg (SRID):    4269
proj4string:    +proj=longlat +ellps=GRS80 +towgs84=0,0,0,0,0,0,0 +no_defs
# Merge with dfmap
state.sf.merged <- merge(state.sf, dfmap, by = "STUSPS", all.y = TRUE)
# Choropleth map
tm_shape(state.sf.merged) +
  tm_basemap("CartoDB.PositronNoLabels") +
  tm_polygons("STUSPS", title = "Average Hate Crime Rate '91-'17", 
              palette = "Oranges", border.col = "Black", border.alpha = 0.25,
              id="STUSPS")
